features.summary: Apple reported Q1 Fiscal Year 2023 revenue of $117.2 billion, down 5% year-over-year, impacted by foreign exchange headwinds, COVID-19-related supply challenges, and a difficult macroeconomic environment. iPhone revenue was $65.8 billion, down 8% year-over-year, while services revenue reached an all-time high of $20.8 billion, up 6%. The installed base of active devices grew to over 2 billion, reflecting strong customer loyalty and satisfaction.
features.management_outlook: Looking ahead, Apple expects Q2 revenue performance to be similar to Q1, with continued foreign exchange headwinds. Services revenue is anticipated to grow year-over-year, while iPhone revenue is expected to accelerate. Mac and iPad revenues are projected to decline due to challenging comparisons and macroeconomic pressures. Gross margin is expected to be between 43.5% and 44.5%.
features.risks: Apple faces risks from ongoing foreign exchange volatility, macroeconomic challenges, and potential supply chain disruptions. The company is also navigating the impacts of COVID-19, which could affect production and demand. Additionally, the uncertain global economic environment may influence consumer spending behavior.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of Apple's performance include the growth of its installed base, which supports services revenue, and the strong demand for iPhone despite supply constraints. The introduction of new products, such as the M2-powered MacBook Pro and iPad, has also contributed to revenue growth. Customer engagement with services continues to rise, with over 935 million paid subscriptions, enhancing the overall ecosystem.
